stem: A industrial safety safety system uses 2 parallel sensors for redundancy. The hazard detected requires at least one sensor to function. sensor 1 has a reliability between 74% and 84% (uncertain due to limited operational data). sensor 2 has a reliability of 94%. What is the overall probability that the hazard detected?

ground_truth.answer: P(hazard detected) ranges from 0.9844 to 0.9904. Point estimate: 0.9874.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 2 sensors fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.26 × 0.06 = 0.0156. P(works) = 0.9844.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.16 × 0.06 = 0.0096. P(works) = 0.9904.
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
